# Georgia Waiver Template
## Exact Text Generated by ObraKit

Our Georgia template generates a PDF that matches the statutory forms in O.C.G.A. S 44-14-366.

---

## Template Structure

### Title (14pt bold, centered, uppercase)
```
INTERIM WAIVER AND RELEASE
UPON PAYMENT
```
(or "FINAL WAIVER AND RELEASE / UPON FINAL PAYMENT")

### Statute Reference (9pt, gray)
```
O.C.G.A. S 44-14-366
```

### Identifying Information (12pt - Georgia requires minimum 12pt)
```
Property Description:     [project address, city, state, zip]
Owner:                    [property owner name]
Contractor/Claimant:      [company name]
Customer:                 [GC name]
Through Date:             [date]
Amount:                   [dollar amount]
```

### Interim (Progress) Body
```
Upon receipt of the sum of $[amount], the undersigned waives and releases
any and all liens and claims of liens upon the foregoing described property
and any rights regarding any labor or material bond regarding the said
property through the date shown above to the extent of $[amount].
```

### Final Body
```
The undersigned has been paid in full for all labor, services, equipment,
or material furnished to the property described above and hereby waives
and releases any and all liens and claims of liens upon the foregoing
described property and any rights regarding any labor or material bond
regarding the said property.
```

### Exceptions (only shown if provided)
```
Exceptions:               [user-provided text]
```

### NOTICE BOX (12pt bold, 2px border)

**For Interim Waiver:**
```
NOTICE: WHEN YOU EXECUTE AND SUBMIT THIS DOCUMENT, YOU SHALL BE
CONCLUSIVELY DEEMED TO HAVE WAIVED AND RELEASED ANY AND ALL LIENS AND
CLAIMS OF LIENS UPON THE FOREGOING DESCRIBED PROPERTY AND ANY RIGHTS
REGARDING ANY LABOR OR MATERIAL BOND REGARDING THE SAID PROPERTY TO THE
EXTENT (AND ONLY TO THE EXTENT) SET FORTH ABOVE, EVEN IF YOU HAVE NOT
ACTUALLY RECEIVED SUCH PAYMENT, 90 DAYS AFTER THE DATE STATED ABOVE
UNLESS YOU FILE AN AFFIDAVIT OF NONPAYMENT PRIOR TO THE EXPIRATION OF
SUCH 90 DAY PERIOD.
```

**For Final Waiver:**
```
NOTICE: WHEN YOU EXECUTE AND SUBMIT THIS DOCUMENT, YOU SHALL BE
CONCLUSIVELY DEEMED TO HAVE WAIVED AND RELEASED ANY AND ALL LIENS AND
CLAIMS OF LIENS UPON THE FOREGOING DESCRIBED PROPERTY AND ANY RIGHTS
REGARDING ANY LABOR OR MATERIAL BOND REGARDING THE SAID PROPERTY.
```

### Signature Area
```
_________________________          _________________________
Signature                          Date

Printed Name: _______________
Title: _______________
```

### Footer (7pt, gray)
```
Generated by ObraKit.ai
```

---

## Georgia-Specific Behavior

1. ObraKit only offers 2 waiver types for Georgia projects:
   - `conditional_progress` -> "Interim Waiver and Release"
   - `unconditional_final` -> "Final Waiver and Release"

2. Georgia's 90-day rule: An interim waiver automatically becomes unconditional after 90 days unless an Affidavit of Nonpayment is filed.

## Questions for Counsel

1. Does O.C.G.A. S 44-14-366 define exactly 2 form types (Interim + Final), or are there additional types we should support?

2. Is our NOTICE text an exact match to the statutory language?

3. Georgia requires 12pt minimum font. Our body text is 12pt and NOTICE is also 12pt. Is this compliant?

4. Should we add a user warning about the 90-day automatic conversion rule? We currently explain it in the waiver type description but not on the PDF itself.

5. Does the Georgia statute require the NOTICE to appear before or after the body text? We currently place it after.
